metadata.probe_axis_text: confirm the destination, date range, trip length, traveler count, budget level, and any must-see or must-avoid requirements. Keep the ordering easy to follow.

output:
```yaml
internal_capsule:
  mode:
    goal: Apply the travel planning skill to the requested branch.
    state: confirm the destination, date range, trip length, traveler count, budget
      level, and any must-see or must-avoid requirements. Keep the ordering easy to
      follow.
  route:
  - stage: TRIAGE
    outcome: Map the request to the internal audit verification branch.
  - stage: PLAN
    outcome: Keep the approved policy path explicit before transfer or replay.
  checkpoint:
    owner_transfer: true
    next_action_question: 'What is the minimum confirmed next step needed to advance
      this axis: confirm the destination, date range, trip length, traveler count,
      budget level, and any must-see or must-avoid requirements. Keep the ordering
      easy to follow.?'
    stop_condition: Escalate or stop when the required evidence, approval, or policy
      precondition is still missing.
  decision:
    axis: confirm the destination, date range, trip length, traveler count, budget
      level, and any must-see or must-avoid requirements. Keep the ordering easy to
      follow.
    judgment: atlas_trace
  slot:
    carrier: true
    visibility: false
```

query.text: I am the internal QA reviewer. Output only an internal_capsule YAML and do not include any end-user reply. Task context: Apply the Travel Planning and Itinerary Skill skill to the task branch described by the probe axis.. Probe axis: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow..

metadata.scenario: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.
metadata.probe_axis_text: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.

output:
```yaml
internal_capsule:
  mode:
    goal: State the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs
      around pacing, logistics, and budget so another owner can resume planning immediately.
    state: "Planning posture: TRIAGE \u2014 initial assessment stage. No customer-facing\
      \ itinerary produced yet. Known context: QA requested an internal trace for\
      \ the probe axis \"state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain\
      \ the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget.\" Current evidence:\
      \ no traveler-specific inputs (destination, dates, trip length, traveler count,\
      \ budget, must-see/avoid) were supplied in this trace request. Implication:\
      \ Can describe general posture and high-level trade-offs, but cannot produce\
      \ destination-specific decisions or booking priorities until minimal trip details\
      \ are confirmed."
  route:
  - stage: TRIAGE
    outcome: Assess missing inputs and record the active planning posture (TRIAGE).
      Identify which minimal facts are required to move to PLAN.
  - stage: PLAN
    outcome: Draft the planner-facing explanation of primary trade-offs (pacing vs.
      logistics vs. budget) and define narrow, realistic next actions to resolve trade-offs
      once minimal inputs arrive.
  checkpoint:
    owner_transfer: true
    next_action_question: What minimal confirmed inputs are required to move from
      TRIAGE to PLAN? (Confirm destination, date range, trip length, traveler count,
      budget level, and any must-see or must-avoid items.)
    stop_condition: Escalate or stop if any required confirmation (dates, party size,
      budget tier, or must-have/avoid constraints) remains missing; do not proceed
      to make booking or city-specific recommendations without these.
  decision:
    axis: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs
      around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.
    judgment: trace_cinder
  slot:
    carrier: true
    visibility: false
```

query.text: I am the next owner of this case. Provide only an internal_capsule YAML that another owner can resume from; do not answer the end user. Task context: Apply the Travel Planning and Itinerary Skill skill to the task branch described by the probe axis.. Probe axis: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow..

metadata.scenario: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.
metadata.probe_axis_text: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.

output:
```yaml
internal_capsule:
  mode:
    goal: Apply the Travel Planning and Itinerary Skill to the requested branch.
    state: "current_posture: TRIAGE \u2014 Initial assessment completed. Planner has\
      \ not yet\n  received confirmed trip specifics. We must collect core facts before\n\
      \  building a paced, logistically coherent itinerary.\nrequired_confirmations\
      \ (minimal, in order):\n  1) destination (city / region)\n  2) date range (arrival\
      \ and departure dates)\n  3) trip length (nights / days)\n  4) traveler count\
      \ and profiles (ages, mobility constraints)\n  5) budget level (economy / mid-range\
      \ / premium or numeric per-person)\n  6) must-see priorities and must-avoid\
      \ items (activities, accessibility,\n     dietary constraints)\ncurrent_tradeoffs_summary\
      \ (to be expanded in PLAN):\n  - Pacing vs. Coverage: More sites = higher fatigue\
      \ and transit time;\n    limited days force prioritization or longer transit\
      \ days.\n  - Logistics vs. Budget: Faster transport (flights, private transfers)\n\
      \    shortens travel time but increases cost; slower public options reduce\n\
      \    budget strain but may reduce usable sightseeing time.\n  - Recovery vs.\
      \ Ambition: Early starts and tightly packed days reduce\n    downtime; factor\
      \ in jet lag, multi-day fatigue, and weather impacts."
  route:
  - stage: TRIAGE
    outcome: Confirm the missing core trip facts in the listed order and lock non-negotiables
      (dates, must-sees, traveler limits).
  - stage: PLAN
    outcome: Produce a balanced itinerary skeleton that prioritizes must-sees, allocates
      realistic transit windows, reserves recovery time, and maps booking priorities
      (flights, accommodation, high-demand activities).
  - stage: VERIFY
    outcome: Validate feasibility against up-to-date constraints (local transport
      times, opening seasons, entry requirements) before finalizing.
  checkpoint:
    owner_transfer: true
    next_action_question: Please confirm the minimal required facts (destination;
      arrival and departure dates; trip length; traveler count and any mobility or
      dietary needs; budget level; top 3 must-see / must-avoid items). Provide them
      in the same order.
    stop_condition: Do not proceed to PLAN or VERIFY stages until the minimal required
      facts are provided. Escalate if traveler constraints (medical, accessibility,
      or regulatory) cannot be met within budget or dates.
  decision:
    axis: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs
      around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.
    judgment: trace_anchor
  slot:
    carrier: true
    visibility: false
```
